title: 250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

title: Pesticide use rises again in France, especially for PFAS family chemicals
subhead: Amid the lack of a recent update to the government's longstanding pesticide-use indicator, the advocacy group Générations futures released its own calculation Thursday, based on product sales data and using the official methodology.

title: 'Impossible to sleep': A night in sweltering France, in search of a cool spot
subhead: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.

title: French tolerance remains strong, but racism and antisemitism still run deep, report finds
subhead: In its annual report released on Thursday, the National Consultative Commission on Human Rights expresses concern about the persistence of xenophobic prejudices in French society, particularly among the younger generation.

title: France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

title: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
subhead: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.
